context.setting: Working on laptop with laptop on Left

episode_text:
day=7; meal=chicken nuggets, broccoli, and ketchup; setting=Working on laptop with laptop on Left; time_of_day=morning;
preferences: microwave_time=2 min; occlusion_relevance=do not consider occlusion; robot_speed=medium; skewering_axis=parallel to major axis; web_interface_confirmation=yes; transfer_mode=outside mouth transfer; outside_mouth_distance=near; convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transfer=LED; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_feeding=button; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_drinking=button; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_wiping=button; convey_robot_ready_for_completing_transfer=LED; detect_user_completed_transfer_feeding=perception; detect_user_completed_transfer_drinking=button; detect_user_completed_transfer_wiping=button; retract_between_bites=no; bite_dipping_preference=less; wait_before_autocontinue_seconds=1000 sec

microwave_time.user_tendencies: IF dining context is social with a person on the user's right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a hot savory meal, THEN 2 min BECAUSE the user selected longer reheating for chicken and hash browns in that observed context. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet in front or on the left AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a hot savory meal, THEN 2 min BECAUSE the user repeatedly chose longer reheating for savory meals while working, including fish bites with potatoes, buffalo chicken bites with wedges, and chicken nuggets with broccoli. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is cold fruit, THEN no microwave BECAUSE the user explicitly chose not to microwave fruit-only items intended to be served cold. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a mixed sweet meal including brownies with sauce, THEN 2 min BECAUSE the user selected reheating for bananas, brownies, and chocolate sauce, indicating that dessert-like items may still be preferred warm.

occlusion_relevance.user_tendencies: IF dining context includes watching TV on the user's right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN minimize right occlusion BECAUSE the user explicitly chose to preserve visibility toward the right-side TV. IF dining context is social with a person on the user's right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN minimize right occlusion BECAUSE the user prioritized preserving visibility toward the right side when attention to a right-side partner likely mattered. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN minimize front occlusion BECAUSE the user selected preserving visibility toward the front while working in that configuration. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN minimize front occlusion BECAUSE this was selected across multiple front-TV meals. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet on the user's left AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN do not consider occlusion BECAUSE this was selected repeatedly in left-laptop working meals, suggesting occlusion concerns are strongest for front and right attention targets rather than universally for all attended directions.

robot_speed.user_tendencies: IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is cold fruit, THEN fast BECAUSE the user explicitly selected fast speed in that easy-to-serve, low-risk context. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a mixed dessert meal with sauce, THEN medium BECAUSE the user selected a more moderate speed for a messier meal. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is hot savory food, THEN medium BECAUSE the user selected medium speed across observed working meals, including meals with sauce or dip, suggesting a stable balance of efficiency and control in attention-dividing contexts.

skewering_axis.user_tendencies: IF food requires fork acquisition during feeding AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is most solid foods, THEN parallel to major axis BECAUSE the user has limited mouth opening and repeatedly favored narrower bites for savory solids, including another left-laptop working meal with chicken nuggets and broccoli. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is soft dessert items such as brownies with sauce, THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE the user selected perpendicular skewering in that observed meal, suggesting willingness to prioritize bite acquisition success for soft dessert foods.

web_interface_confirmation.user_tendencies: IF dining context is social with a person on the user's right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is fork-fed solid food, THEN yes BECAUSE the user explicitly chose confirmation in the observed social meal, consistent with wanting to avoid awkward failed transfers. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet in front or on the left AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is fork-fed solid food, THEN yes BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected confirmation while working, including savory meals with and without sauce, suggesting a stable preference for explicit verification in attention-dividing contexts. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is simple fruit pieces, THEN no BECAUSE the user skipped confirmation in that easier feeding context. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a mixed dessert meal with sauce, THEN yes BECAUSE the user selected confirmation for a more complex, messier bite-acquisition context.

transfer_mode.user_tendencies: IF dining context is a neutral personal meal AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is foods without sauces or dips, THEN outside mouth transfer BECAUSE the user can lean forward comfortably and selected the less intrusive transfer style. IF dining context is social with a person on the user's right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is solid food, THEN outside mouth transfer BECAUSE the user again selected the less intrusive, user-controlled transfer style. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet in front or on the left AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is solid food, THEN outside mouth transfer BECAUSE the user continued to prefer the less intrusive transfer style across working meals. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is fruit pieces or dessert foods, THEN outside mouth transfer BECAUSE the user also selected outside-mouth transfer across front-TV meals.

outside_mouth_distance.user_tendencies: IF transfer_mode is outside mouth transfer AND dining context is a neutral personal meal AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is foods without sauces or dips, THEN near BECAUSE the user selected a close stop distance in the observed personal meal. IF transfer_mode is outside mouth transfer AND dining context is social with a person on the user's right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is solid food, THEN medium BECAUSE the user preferred a somewhat larger buffer in the observed social meal, suggesting more interpersonal comfort distance in social settings. IF transfer_mode is outside mouth transfer AND dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet in front or on the left AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is solid food, THEN near BECAUSE the user selected a close stop distance across working meals, including the repeated left-laptop case. IF transfer_mode is outside mouth transfer AND dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is fruit pieces or dessert foods, THEN near BECAUSE the user consistently selected a close stop distance in front-TV meals.

convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transf.user_tendencies: IF dining context is social with a person on the user's right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN LED BECAUSE the user selected LED in the observed social meal, suggesting a preference for quieter signaling. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet in front or on the left AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN LED BECAUSE the user repeatedly chose LED during working meals, including the repeated left-laptop meal, consistent with low-disruption visual cues during focused activity. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN LED BECAUSE this was selected across front-TV meals, reinforcing a preference for quiet visual cues when attention is directed toward media.

detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfe.user_tendencies: IF dining context involves possible distraction, conversation, or focused activity AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN button BECAUSE the user has reliable arm control, limited mouth opening makes open-mouth readiness less attractive, and the observed social, TV, and working meals consistently favored explicit button control.

IF dining context involves possible distraction, conversation, or focused activity AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN button BECAUSE the user can reliably press buttons and consistently preferred direct manual control in the observed meals.

IF dining context involves possible distraction, conversation, or focused activity AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN button BECAUSE the user can reliably press buttons and consistently preferred direct manual control in the observed meals.

convey_robot_ready_for_completing_transf.user_tendencies: IF dining context is social with a person on the user's right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN LED BECAUSE the user selected LED in the observed social meal, suggesting a preference for quieter completion cues. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet in front or on the left AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN LED BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected LED during working meals, including the repeated left-laptop meal. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN LED BECAUSE this was selected across front-TV meals, reinforcing a preference for quiet visual completion cues.

detect_user_completed_transfer_feeding.user_tendencies: IF task is feeding AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN perception BECAUSE the user consistently chose the reliable, low-effort completion detection method.

detect_user_completed_transfer_drinking.user_tendencies: IF task is drinking AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN button BECAUSE the user consistently preferred explicit manual completion signaling over automatic continuation.

detect_user_completed_transfer_wiping.user_tendencies: IF task is mouth wiping AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN button BECAUSE the user consistently preferred explicit manual completion signaling over automatic continuation.

retract_between_bites.user_tendencies: IF dining context includes watching TV on the user's right AND affective_state is neutral AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN yes BECAUSE the user chose to reduce prolonged visual obstruction in a context where right-side visibility matters. IF dining context is social with a person on the user's right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN yes BECAUSE the user again prioritized reducing prolonged obstruction when attention to the right side was important. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN yes BECAUSE the user selected retraction while working in that front-facing configuration. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN yes BECAUSE this was selected across front-TV meals. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet on the user's left AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN no BECAUSE this was selected repeatedly in left-laptop working meals, suggesting the user does not want the extra time cost of retracting when left-side work does not make obstruction especially problematic.

bite_dipping_preference.user_tendencies: IF food includes a dip or sauce AND affective_state is neutral AND dining context involves watching TV in front AND food_condition is simple items where dipping is optional, THEN do not dip BECAUSE the user explicitly declined dipping in one observed front-TV meal despite sauce availability, showing a conservative baseline. IF food includes a dip or sauce AND dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet in front or on the left AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified, THEN less BECAUSE the user selected some dipping but not maximal dipping across working meals with tartar sauce, ranch dressing, and ketchup, suggesting a moderate, mess-conscious preference in task-focused contexts. IF food includes a dip or sauce AND dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a dessert meal with chocolate sauce, THEN less BECAUSE the user selected some dipping for bananas, brownies, and chocolate sauce, indicating moderate dipping when the sauce is integral to the meal. IF food_condition is a meal without dips or sauces AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND dining context is any observed context, THEN do not dip BECAUSE dipping is unnecessary and the user's observed choices remain conservative.

wait_before_autocontinue_seconds.user_tendencies: IF dining context is social with a person on the user's right AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN 1000 sec BECAUSE the user selected a very long wait in the observed social meal, indicating a strong preference to avoid unwanted automatic progression when social interaction may delay responses. IF dining context involves working on a laptop/tablet in front or on the left AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is any food condition, THEN 1000 sec BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected a very long wait while working, including the repeated left-laptop meal, suggesting a stable preference to prevent automatic progression when attention is divided. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is simple fruit pieces, THEN 10 sec BECAUSE the user selected the short wait in that easier front-TV meal. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective_state is neutral/unspecified AND food_condition is a mixed dessert meal with sauce, THEN 1000 sec BECAUSE the user selected a very long wait in that more complex front-TV meal, indicating that in TV contexts the wait preference depends on meal complexity.
